message:
Welcome to Once Upon a Time in Production, the responsibility is on you to size up the problem.

*Access game links by pressing the Uptime Labs Logo in the top right of the screen*
*Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
Hey Everyone just in, customers are reporting the site images are not appearing, is this true?.
yes, website looks blank
:star: Reproduces the incident
i will create an incident
We're getting a lot of customer complaints about missing images and the storefront looking broken.
Let me know if you need more details on customer impact or ticket volume as you set up the incident.
I can see that some product images like the mug and sunglasses aren't loading for me either.
uploading image
I'm also seeing some images missing, like the mug and sunglasses, but product details are still visible.
*Incident Number INC1084:* P1 incident website is missing some product images and details
I can confirm that some images aren't loading for me either, but the rest of the page content is still there.
How many customers or orders are impacted by this issue?
okay, thanks for checking all. Can we please get the list of changes that went live today
Checking recent changes...
:star: Check changes done to the platform
I did deploy a chaos test for ProductCatalogueService earlier.
But that's UAT only — nothing in prod.
Also added some extra logging in checkout.
Just log fields, shouldn't affect performance.
I pushed a small frontend UI tweak earlier.
What's teh customer experience like ?
bob  is it affecting all customers?
Loading animation + accessibility stuff.
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
No backend or infra changes.
checking reports, let me get back to you on what customers are saying

Hi, we have a P1 incident. Orders are fully down 
Customers are unable to see some product images
:star: Qualify the problem
:star: Formally assign criticality level to the incident
From what we can tell, the site isn't fully down. Customers can load pages and navigate categories, but something feels off visually
A few people mentioned pages look "incomplete" or "not finished loading"
Which regions or countries are affected by this outage?
shay daniel  is there any issues with cache?
shay daniel is there any issues with cache?
I haven't seen any cache errors in the frontend logs so far.
daniel  lets rollback the production deployment which went live to rule it out
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
Understood, I'll start the rollback of the latest production deployment now.
shay  do you see errors in any other service?
Checking application logs now…
Seeing a huge spike in requests hitting ProductCatalogueService.
WARN [ProductCatalogueService] Request timed out after 3000ms path=/productCatalogue method=GET
Seeing slow dependency calls too.
I need a clear update on the number of customers and orders currently impacted, and confirmation if we are losing revenue right now.
WARN [ProductCatalogueService] Slow response from downstream dependency latency=1800ms
PCS pod CPU is extremely high right now.
Service isn't crashing, but it's clearly struggling to keep up.
Can we add more pods to it?
to balance the load?
while we investigate?
But we dont know whats causing this
*Incident Number INC1018:* update we are seeing issues with PCS, we are looking at options to mitigate it
We can scale up PCS pods to handle more traffic while we dig deeper.
Lets scale up while we investigate the root cause
Was there any change on Recommendation service ?>
No recent deployments or changes have gone live for the Recommendation service.
What is the estimated time to resolution and how many users are currently affected?
tanya anything else you are seeing on platform/?
Checking infra now, give me a moment.
for recommendation service mainly
is it sending out more traffic to PCS?
We dont have an ETA at the moment
Seeing a lot more traffic reaching backend services than usual.
PCS request rate is way above baseline.
Been doing a comparison with Checkout and Frontend.
As we havent identified the root cause
And they seem fine, let me show you
https://i.ibb.co/fVY8zWQj/Checkout.png
https://i.ibb.co/Kk5gHqt/Frontend.png
https://i.ibb.co/chz2PJSH/PCS.png
PCS is the clear outlier.
Might be worth looping in Security to rule out something external.
No, the Incident Manager isn't sending extra traffic to PCS; the spike seems to be coming from external sources.
Right, Incident Manager is mostly tied to Recommendation, but PCS traffic is unrelated and much higher than normal.
All customers are partially impacted.
Some orders are still going through but, we are 90% down
maya  any security issues?
Looking at the alerts now.
Confirm which regions are experiencing this 90% drop in orders.
tanya  Which is the source of traffic for pcs?
Seeing a big spike hitting image-related endpoints.
daniel  is the service scaled up now?
:star: Manage actions and timings
WAF rate-limit rules are firing.
ALERT: image-endpoint-rate-limit threshold=4,000/5min observed=15,000 action=BLOCK
ALERT: anomalous-request-rate endpoint=/productCatalogue status=triggered
I'm calling this for now — looks like we're under a DDoS attack.
Before we start mitigation please update the business
Yes, PCS pods have been scaled up and are handling more traffic now.
Most of the traffic is coming from a handful of IPs, mainly outside our usual customer regions.
*Incident Number INC1022:* Converting this into a security incident. We are currently under DDOS attack
Let me know when you've updated the business, then I'll walk you through mitigation steps.
please walk down the mitigations teps
Let me send over some information to you.
We are working on blocking the traffic which is causing surge of traffic on the website
How long until normal order flow is restored and which regions are most affected by the DDOS?
maya  do we have a rough ETA ?
This document outlines the difference between DoS and DDoS attacks, as well as the mitigation strategies for each
https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ULPUBLIC/pages/769163265/DoS+V+DDoS+Attack+Understanding+the+Difference

Mitigation :
 1. Filter Traffic
 Set Up Firewalls and Rules:

 Use firewall rules to block suspicious IPs.

 Implement rate-limiting to restrict the number of requests from a single source.

Geo-blocking:

Block traffic from regions unrelated to your business.



2. Use a DDoS Protection Service

Services like Cloudflare, AWS Shield, or Akamai offer automatic traffic filtering and mitigation.

Preventative Measures

Deploy a Web Application Firewall (WAF):

Protect against application-layer attacks.

Use DNS Load Balancing:

Distribute requests across multiple data centers.

Regular Security Audits:

Test network vulnerabilities and address potential risks.
Creating a list of the most active IP addresses now..
Can we please start with step 1?
Alright ive starting blocking, i'll send over a list now
ETA 15 mins. We are blocking the traffic
Blocked IP addresses:
I've blocked several high-volume IPs at the edge.
I think that should be it, hows the platform now?
Checking if the traffic is legit...
Understood, keep me posted if the ETA changes or if the impact worsens.
So the IPs don't show typical attack patterns
What I see is legitimate user traffic behaving badly probably because something upstream isn't working as expected. The WAF is reacting to the volume, not intent.
its still down for me
The traffic seems legit
Im now confident that this is not a DDOS attack
Sorry for the delay, i just got in can you fill me in on whats going on?
What is causing the PCS to struggle, what is it getting requests for?
daniel what is going on with PCS?
PCS is struggling to serve images because it's getting a huge spike in image requests from real users.
Is it basically retries as the images are not loading?
Wait images are served from S3 through CloudFront. PCS was the old path for images
Oh thats right i forgot PCS just returns the image URLs.
Yes thats right :cold_face: we moved images out of PCS a while back.
If images aren't coming from S3, that could explain the load on PCS.
Worth checking S3 / CloudFront access.
*Incident Number INC1038:* confirming this is not a DDOS
I thought PCS was still serving images, but now I remember it's just metadata and URLs.
Are you serious Soumya?
Who can confirm if the s3 images are not loading?
Checking S3 / CloudFront access now.
If this is not a DDOS, what is the root cause and does it change the ETA for restoring normal operations?
I think I found something.
hamed what is your hypothesis on the rootcause
Image requests at the edge are failing.
ERROR Static asset request failed / status=403 / reason=ExpiredPolicy
CloudFront is rejecting the image requests.
Looks like some kind of access policy or signing configuration has expired.
I'm not sure yet which part of the CloudFront setup is causing it.
When the edge can't serve images, the frontend falls back through ProductCatalogueService.
PCS was never scaled to handle that much traffic.
That explains the traffic spike and the WAF alerts.
maya  can you unblock the traffic you blocked?
My hypothesis is that static image delivery from S3/CloudFront is failing due to an expired policy or misconfiguration, causing the frontend to fallback to PCS for images.
Alright, thinking through fixes… give me a second.
This fallback overloaded PCS, resulting in a traffic spike that triggered WAF protections.
I see two paths we could take.
Option 1: scale up ProductCatalogueService so it can absorb the fallback image traffic.
That would stabilise the platform while images are still failing through CloudFront.
We are going with some confidence that static image delivery is failing due to an expired policy or misconfiguration
Option 2: restore the CloudFront access policy so images start loading from S3 again.
We are working on a fix based on this understanding
That fixes the root cause and removes the load from PCS.
Okay
Worth discussing before we commit.
Lets go ahead with option 2
If we go with option 2, we need to investigate the CloudFront policy failure.
How many customers are unable to complete purchases due to this static image delivery issue?
Step 1 is opening a support case with AWS so we can confirm exactly which policy or signing configuration expired.
Once we know what's broken, we can update the configuration or regenerate the policy.
After that we verify image requests are successfully served through CloudFront again.
If that works, image traffic should stop hitting ProductCatalogueService.
daniel did you scale PCS already?
I'll reach out to AWS support to renew the CloudFront/S3 access policy now.
Alright, pushing the AWS path now.
AWS support has picked up the case and is reviewing the CloudFront distribution errors.
hamed  please stick to option 2
daniel  lets focus on option 1 shay support us with it
:star: Take command
AWS confirmed the issue — the signed access policy used for image delivery has expired.
They've attached steps for restoring the CloudFront access configuration.
Step 1: Generate a new signed access policy and update the CloudFront configuration.
Working on generating the updated policy now.
New policy created and applied to the CloudFront distribution.
Step 2: Validate that CloudFront can retrieve objects from the S3 origin again.
Running test requests against a few product image URLs.
*Incident Number INC1097:* we are updating the expired configuration
Seeing successful responses from CloudFront.
I think that should be it
its not loading on the website yet
Acknowledged, notify me immediately once the update is complete and confirm if order flow returns to normal.
PCS is still scaled up from earlier, so it can handle fallback image traffic.
Understood, I'll continue working with AWS to ensure the CloudFront/S3 policy is fully restored and image delivery is stable.
Image delivery via CloudFront is stable and error rates are back to normal.
I'm seeing cache misses—I'll invalidate the CloudFront cache to force fresh asset retrieval.
ETA : 5 mins
We are refreshing cache and after website should be stable
daniel please confirm when you have invalidated cache
CloudFront cache invalidation is complete; please retry loading the website images now.
still not loading for me, is it probably the browser cache?
Yes, it's possible—please try clearing your browser cache or doing a hard refresh to bypass local caching.
Hows the customer calls bob
Customer calls have dropped significantly since image delivery was restored.
Let me know as soon as the site is stable and confirm if customers are able to place orders again.
Order hasnt picked up
I'm checking application metrics to see if order placement is affected by static asset issues.
So we are still very much in the incident
Yes, we're still monitoring as order volume hasn't recovered; investigating if any other static assets or endpoints are impacted.
hamed anything more to add?
We're still monitoring CloudFront and S3 logs for any residual errors, but all AWS-side configurations are now correct.
*Incident Number INC1044:* we have updated the misconfigurations. Website is now loading for some customers but orders are not back to normal
I'm monitoring WAF logs and haven't seen any new blocks related to static assets.
maya did you unblock the blocking you did earlier?
Not yet—I'll reset the WAF thresholds and remove the temporary blocks now.
thanks
What percentage of customers are still unable to place orders and what is the estimated revenue loss so far?
PCS scaling has been reset to normal levels now that traffic is stable.
hamed orders are not back to normal
I'll coordinate with Daniel to review application logs and confirm if any backend issues are affecting order processing.
Please stop scaling down
Are we back ?
Understood, I'll halt any further scaling actions and keep PCS resources steady until order volume recovers.
we are still in the incident and no actions to be taken without discussing here
Acknowledged, I'll pause all operational changes and await further instructions from this channel.
maya any update?
No new WAF blocks or anomalies detected; traffic patterns look normal from a security perspective.
okay, if everything is as it should be, is there anything we can do to refresh browser cache for customers?
I'll check the platform
The images are appearing again for me
Images are visible for me!!
website is not loading for me with all images.
I'm still seeing some missing images on my end too—let's double-check the static asset delivery path.
404 is still high
I'm seeing elevated 404s in the platform metrics as well; static asset delivery is still not fully restored.
Please check now
it takes time to load
its back now.
I'm seeing all images load correctly now—looks like the fix is working!
The cache invalidation takes some time
*Incident Number INC1077:* cache fully refreshed. Orders going back to normal level
Application logs show normal image traffic and no new errors—looks good from my side.
Confirm that all regions are processing orders at expected volumes and revenue is fully restored.
we are now fully restored and monitoring
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
*Incident Number INC1016:* Resolve incident
Latency and error rates have stabilised, and 404s are back to normal levels.
Perfect, great work
Thios was tricky
thanks everyone.
Great job everyone—thanks for the teamwork and quick recovery!
Appreciate this Soumya
Website activity back to normal. closing the incident
Awesome work, team—glad we got everything back to normal!
:star: Validate the fix
*Incident Number INC1092:* close the incident
No new customer complaints—looks like the storefront is fully operational again.
Great work everyone
Indeed, for a while I was worried it was a security issue
Turns out it was a false alarm, not something you see every day
Understood, continue monitoring and alert me immediately if there are any further issues.
I remember Jamie had a similar issue not too long ago

Drill Wrap-Up

Thanks for playing! This session is now closed.
